metadata.title: 14. Delhi Sultanate: Architecture, Arts and Culture

questions.question: Which was the first monument in India constructed using Islamic architectural elements including horseshoe-shaped arches?
questions.explanation: The Alai Darwaza, located in Delhi, was the first monument in India built following Islamic architecture principles, including the use of horseshoe-shaped arches. It was constructed in the 14th century by Delhi Sultanate ruler Alauddin Khilji. The Darwaza stands as an entrance to the Qutub complex, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, featuring distinct decorations of inlaid marble and latticed stone screens.

questions.question: "Hasan Nizami" and “Fakh-e-Mudabbir" were the court poets of which ruler of Mamluk Sultanate?
questions.explanation: "Hasan Nizami" and “Fakh-e-Mudabbir" were the court poets of Qutb-ud-din Aybak. Hasan Nizami was a Persian language poet and historian. Fakh-e-Mudabbir was a Persian author and courtier under successive Ghaznavid, Ghūrid, and Shamsī sultans in South Asia.

questions.question: Who wrote famous Hindi work “Padmavat”?
questions.explanation: Padmavat of Malik Muhammad Jayasi is a poem about the beauty and Jauhar of Padmani.

questions.question: Which of the following rulers built the Gujri Mahal in Hissar , Haryana?
questions.explanation: The Gujjari Mahal was built by Firoz Shah Tughlaq for his beloved Gujri Rani, a native of Hisar with whom he fell in love.

questions.question: Who among the following is the Persian mathematician and poet composer of the world famous Rubbayat?
questions.explanation: Rubbayat is written by Omar Khayyam. It is collection ruba i.e .persian poems.
